rulings.Case Title: ALFONSO PAGANO JR ET AL VS. AKEBONO BRAKE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2025 in Department 304, Line 2. Defendant Velan Valve Corp.'s ("Defendant") Motion for Summary Judgement, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. A. Judicial Notice Defendant's request for Judicial Notice of Plaintiffs' Complaint is GRANTED, with the caveat that the Court is not judicially noticing the truth of the allegations therein. (Evid. Code, 452, subds. (c), (d).) B. Defendant's Evidentiary Objections Defendant's objections in reply are OVERRULED. C. Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence of asbestos exposure and causation. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant argues that Plaintiff lacks evidence to establish that he was exposed to asbestos from a product for which Defendant was liable. The Court disagrees. For the reasons set forth below, the Court finds that Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden. Further, Plaintiff provides sufficient evidence in opposition to establish triable issues of material fact with respect to exposure, causation and punitive damages. *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel (Transaction ID # 76113351). The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)

rulings.Case Title: JANICE M. COOK ET AL VS. ANNING-JOHNSON COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2025 in Department 304, Line 4. Defendant Sequoia Ventures Inc's ("Defendant") Motion for Summary Judgment or, in the Alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication, is GRANTED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. A. Defendant's Evidentiary Objections / The Court declines to rule on Defendant's Objections No. 1-4 to the Declaration of Dr. Michael Ellenbecker, attached as Exhibit 3 to Plaintiffs' Index of Exhibits, as the Court did not rely on the referenced evidence for the reasons set forth below. B. Motion for Summary Judgment / Defendant brings its summary judgment motion on two grounds. First, Defendant argues Plaintiffs insufficiently alleged they were exposed to asbestos because of work that Bechtel workers performed in the vicinity of Mr. Cook. (Motion, 11, 16.) Second, Defendant alternatively argues that the Privette doctrine shields Defendant from liability. (Motion, 20.) The Court agrees that the general rule of nonliability established in Privette applies here, and grants summary judgment on that basis. Accordingly, the Court declines to rule on the asbestos exposure issue. C. Motion for Summary Adjudication / Plaintiffs do not oppose Defendant's alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication as to their Third Cause of Action for Negligent Misrepresentation, Fourth Cause of Action for Fraud by Nondisclosure, and punitive damages claim. Defendant's Motion is therefore GRANTED as to those claims. *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel (Transaction ID # 76113395). The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)

rulings.Case Title: JANICE M. COOK ET AL VS. ANNING-JOHNSON COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2025 in Department 304, Line 4. DEFENDANT RTX CORPORATION, F/K/A RAYTHEON TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION F/K/A UNITED TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Opposition Filed. Reply Filed. A. Evidentiary Objections a. Plaintiff's Objections / Plaintiff's objections to the Declaration of Jack Sumner are SUSTAINED IN PART on the grounds of hearsay and lack of foundation. (Code Civ. Proc. 2025.620; Evid. Code. 1200, 1291(a), 1292(a), 401-403, and 702.) Mr. Sumner, a longtime engineer at Pratt & Whitney, provides no foundation regarding his opinions regarding Plaintiff Lowrey's ("Mr. Lowrey") work activities, exposure, and the presence of asbestos containing materials during Mr. Lowrey's employment at the United Airlines Maintenance Base. Mr. Sumner is not a corporate representative of United Airlines. Moreover, he admits that "I do not know and have never met Plaintiffs or Mr. Lowrey, have never observed Mr. Lowrey perform any work on aviation engines, and have no personal knowledge of any work that Mr. Lowrey performed at any time with any product." (Sumner. Decl. at 4.) Mr. Sumner's opinion that Mr. Lowrey did not work with asbestos-containing components designed, manufactured, or supplied by Pratt & Whitney is speculative and inadmissible. (See Evid. Code 702(a); LAOSD Asbestos Cases (Ramirez v. Avon) (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 939, 947.) b. Defendant's Objections / Defendant's objections to the Declaration of Michael Ellenbecker are OVERRULED. Mr. Ellenbecker's declaration shows he is an industrial hygienist and has reviewed documents and information regarding equipment manufactured and sold by Defendant. Mr. Ellenbecker's expertise qualifies him to testify as an expert. Questions about the depth or scope of his knowledge or expertise go to the weight, not the admissibility, of his testimony. *Complete tentative ruling has been electronically served to Counsel (Transaction ID # 76113395). The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)
